note: Sprint 5.30 Trinity Task Builder Reader Metadata v0.1: extends scripts/trinity/useful_compute_task_builder.py so the --from-scientific-intake bridge writes a new metadata.scientific_reader_manifest block into the Useful Compute request alongside the Sprint 5.21 metadata.scientific_intake block. The manifest carries per-document path_basename, sha256 (raw bytes), reader_kind (text/json/pdf/latex/csv/unsupported), reader_status (ok/unsupported_extension/unsupported_missing_dependency/parse_error), extracted_text_sha256 (64-hex of the reader-extracted plain text), structured_summary (reader-specific small dict) and per-doc warnings, plus reader_kind_counts and reader_status_counts roll-ups and intake_warnings carried over from the intake top-level. The raw extracted text NEVER reaches the request — only its sha256. The Sprint 5.20 text_preview and the Sprint 5.29 extracted_text_preview ALSO never reach the request. Absolute filesystem paths NEVER reach the request — only basenames. Schemas/trinity/useful_compute_request.schema.json gains a new OPTIONAL scientific_reader_manifest property under metadata; request schema $id stays trinity-useful-compute-request/v0.1 — this is an additive change, pre-Sprint-5.30 requests without the field still validate. The reader-sensitivity chain established in Sprint 5.29 flows through to the worker unchanged: extracted_text_sha256 (5.29) -> combined_context_sha256 (5.29 mix-in) -> input_bundle_sha256 (5.21 bridge) -> compute_output_sha256 (5.12+ worker seed). A regression test mutates a document body between two intake -> builder -> worker runs and asserts the worker's compute_output_sha256 differs, proving the chain stays intact end-to-end. The worker is silent on the new field — its pre-Sprint-5.30 validation of metadata.scientific_intake is untouched, and the new sibling field is allowed by the request schema but not consumed by worker logic. The 5.21 anti-leak test test_request_does_not_copy_document_content was narrowed: still bans the document body, still bans text_preview, now ALSO bans extracted_text_preview, no longer bans path_basename (which is deliberately exposed by the Sprint 5.30 manifest as a public identifier). _per_doc_reader_record handles pre-5.29 intakes gracefully by defaulting reader_kind=text, reader_status=ok, extracted_text_sha256=sha256('') so the request schema still validates against legacy intakes. Tests: 1204 passed, 38 skipped on the deployment host (1191 prior + 13 new = 13 functional/schema; one extra functional test still pytest-skipped when pypdf/PyPDF2 not installed). Manual demo: build a request from the Sprint 5.29 5-document intake; resulting metadata.scientific_reader_manifest shows documents_count=5, reader_kind_counts={text:2, csv:1, latex:1, pdf:1}, reader_status_counts={ok:4, unsupported_missing_dependency:1}, plus the PDF missing-dep warning carried over in intake_warnings. The same request is drop-in compatible with useful_compute_operator_loop.py --request-json and with task_queue.py enqueue / run-once / run-batch — both continue to complete with governor_decisions_count=7 and watchdog_safety_status=ok. Non-goals for v0.1 (documented): no extracted text in request, no extracted_text_preview in request, no new worker logic (compute_output_sha256 formula unchanged), no schema version bump, no source-tool expansion (only trinity_scientific_prompt_intake produces a manifest), no --show-manifest CLI flag. Pure scripts+schemas+docs+tests merge: zero src/, zero consensus changes, zero wallet/payment/broadcast changes.
